Partner with NICET Level IV Certified Engineers Who Speak Your Language

Complex specifications executed precisely. Code challenges solved collaboratively. CAD drawings, peer review, and technical partnership from design through final inspection.

Engineering Challenges, Solved

Technical projects require technical partners. Here's how FDU delivers engineering excellence:

The Challenge

Contractors who can't execute your specifications precisely

The FDU Solution

NICET IV certified engineers who understand and implement your designs exactly as specified

The Challenge

Poor system design requiring costly change orders

The FDU Solution

Value engineering reviews that optimize constructability without compromising your design intent

The Challenge

Outdated knowledge of current codes and NFPA standards

The FDU Solution

Engineers current on NFPA 72, California codes, and Bay Area AHJ requirements

Collaborative Design-Build Process

From specification review to final inspection—we partner with you at every stage.

01

Specification Review

We review your plans and specifications, ask clarifying questions, and identify constructability opportunities.

02

Collaborative Design

Work directly with NICET IV engineers on value engineering, CAD details, and system optimization.

03

Documentation & Shop Draws

Detailed CAD submittals, riser diagrams, and device schedules that meet your standards.

04

Installation & Verification

Execute your specifications precisely with thorough testing and as-built documentation.

Featured Technical Project

Buck Institute Research Facility, Novato

Complex fire alarm integration for a world-class research facility with specialized detection requirements, redundant systems, and integration with building management and laboratory safety systems.

Technical Questions

Answers for engineering professionals.

Absolutely. We execute engineer specifications precisely, providing detailed submittals that show exactly how we'll implement your design. If we identify constructability improvements, we present them as options—not assumptions—so you maintain design control.
